commit.message:
feat: trie-based leader key system with SPC prefix bindings

Replace flat HashMap<KeyCombo, Action> with KeyTrie (Leaf/Node) in
layer bindings. Multi-key sequences like 'g g' and 'SPC f' are now
stored as trie subtrees and walked one key at a time.

Architecture:
- KeyTrie enum: Leaf(Action) | Node(HashMap<KeyCombo, KeyTrie>)
- Layer::bind_seq("space f", action) creates nested trie nodes
- KeymapSystem stores pending_trie when mid-sequence
- Escape or invalid key cancels pending sequence

Changes:
- Migrated g prefix from transient layer to trie (g g, g _)
- Added leader layer with SPC bindings (SPC f, SPC s, SPC v s, etc.)
- Leader layer active when vim mode is enabled
- Added goto-doc-start and goto-last-non-ws commands
- Added vault-switch and vault-open command dispatch from editor
- 131 tests passing (5 new trie/leader tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add 1-month cooldown to all Dependabot update configurations (#1656)

Dependabot was configured to create PRs immediately after new versions
were published. Adding a 30-day cooldown across all ecosystems reduces
churn from short-lived or unstable releases.

## Changes

- Added `cooldown: default-days: 30` to all five package ecosystems in
`.github/dependabot.yml`:
  - `uv` (backend)
  - `npm` (frontend, docs)
  - `github-actions`
  - `docker`
  - `docker-compose`

```yaml
- package-ecosystem: "uv"
  directory: "/backend"
  schedule:
    interval: "weekly"
  cooldown:
    default-days: 30
```

---------

commit.message:
Fix montage video autostart race condition (#29)

Montage video fails to autostart in certain interaction flows while
individual song playback always works.

**Root cause:** Song autoplay is driven by the `activeSong` render-time
state adjustment which directly sets `playing = true`. Montage autoplay
relies solely on detecting a `tvPlayback({ command: "play" })` change —
which silently no-ops when the previous value was already `{ command:
"play" }` from an earlier interaction.

**Fix:** Add a `tvMode` transition handler mirroring the existing
`activeSong` pattern, so entering montage mode deterministically starts
playback:

```tsx
const [prevTvMode, setPrevTvMode] = useState<"song" | "montage" | null>(null);
if (tvMode !== prevTvMode) {
  setPrevTvMode(tvMode);
  if (tvMode === "montage") {
    setPlaying(true);
  }
}
```

This makes montage autoplay independent of the `tvPlayback` channel's
state history.

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: 8-agent audit — harden learning + coordinator + automation

Learning system:
- Take only LAST override per cycle per night (was double-counting rapid overrides)
- Parameterize SQL query (was using % string interpolation)
- Filter NULL settings in query
- Close cursor after fetch
- Validate and clamp _load_learned (reject non-dict, enforce ±3 bounds)

fix: prevent streaming restart loop on DLNA players

Three-part fix for feedback loop where Ynison echo → false seek → stream
restart → repeat every ~3.5 seconds on DLNA players (APrender):

1. Guard select_source on _active_player_id (set immediately) instead of
   in_use_by (set after ~5s DLNA negotiation) to prevent redundant calls

2. Echo-aware seek detection: _is_ynison_echo() filters our own progress
   values reflected back by Ynison; _streaming_progress_ms (byte counter)
   used as source of truth instead of wall-clock estimation

3. Grace period (_seek_grace_until) applied on both track changes AND
   seeks to suppress false drift detection during stream restart

Also centralizes all update_playing_status calls through
_send_progress_to_ynison() wrapper for consistent echo tracking.

commit.message:
feat: add possibility to amend configuration in a plugin (#5124)

We have a `preProcess` plugin that needs to store some information for
later use when a document is rendered. Currently, it is not possible to
update the `initialUserConfig` config object, since any update to the
`config` object is ignored. These updates in plugins are visible to
other plugins, but not to the rendered document.

There is a way to update the object with `"amend-user-config"`, but that
requires publishing to the internal system. That system isn't accessible
to a plugin.

Therefore, add a new method that can be called with an `Object` that
forwards it to the internal publication system. That way, a plugin can
update configuration (like some of the core plugins do) and update the
`initialUserConfig`.

commit.message:
fix: call test-associate after key exchange and handle broadcast messages (#2)

Three protocol-level fixes:

1. _ensure_session() now calls test_associate() after change_public_keys().
   KeePassXC resets its internal m_associated flag on every key exchange, so
   authenticated requests (get-logins, set-login, lock-database, etc.) all
   return error 8 (Association failed) unless test-associate is sent first.
   A new _associated bool tracks this per-session state.

2. _send_json() now uses JSONDecoder.raw_decode() instead of json.loads() to
   parse only the first JSON object from the recv buffer. KeePassXC sends an
   unsolicited 'database-locked' broadcast immediately after the lock-database
   response, producing concatenated JSON that json.loads() rejects with
   'Extra data'.

3. Error responses in _send_encrypted() are now logged at WARNING level
   (was DEBUG) so callers see the actual KeePassXC error message.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename WI_ platform API to ax equivalents

Replace platform-specific WI_* symbols with the new ax* / AX_* names across the codebase. Changes include renaming event types (WI_Message -> AXmessage), key/modifier constants (WI_KEY_ -> AX_KEY_, WI_MOD_ -> AX_MOD_), timing and window APIs (WI_GetMilliseconds, WI_Init, WI_Shutdown, WI_CreateWindow, WI_BeginPaint, WI_EndPaint, WI_PollEvent, WI_WaitEvent, WI_PostMessageW, etc.) to their ax counterparts, plus related scaling/size/joystick functions. Updated documentation, examples, tests, kernel, commctl, and user modules to match the new platform API naming.

* Fix WinAPI name conflicts and Windows test crash

- commctl/msgbox.h: wrap MB_OK/OKCANCEL/YESNO/YESNOCANCEL and IDOK/CANCEL/YES/NO in #ifndef guards to suppress redefinition warnings when compiled alongside <winuser.h>
- user/accel.h: wrap FVIRTKEY/FSHIFT/FCONTROL/FALT in #ifndef guards (same reason)
- kernel/init.c: add g_graphics_initialized flag; guard ui_begin_frame()/ui_end_frame() so they're no-ops when platform not initialized, preventing crash in headless unit tests (button_click_test, toolbar_*_test)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/corepunch/orion-ui/sessions/c190dd6d-33d6-426a-94a2-01d9b75673b6

Co-authored-by: corepunch <83646194+corepunch@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Windows test crash: skip paint messages in repost_messages() when !running

create_window() calls invalidate_window() which posts kWindowMessagePaint
and kWindowMessageNonClientPaint. When repost_messages() dispatches these
to window procs (e.g. win_button), they call fill_rect()/draw_text_small()
which use OpenGL function pointers. On Windows without glewInit() these
pointers are NULL, causing a crash with no output.

Fix: skip both paint message types in repost_messages() when !running
(consistent with existing kWindowMessageRefreshStencil guard).

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/corepunch/orion-ui/sessions/e20d4acb-77f3-44bf-8b71-3e7ca3b9b7b1

Co-authored-by: corepunch <83646194+corepunch@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reset UI state and deduplicate posted messages

Add test_env_reset_ui_state to clear global UI state (destroy windows, cleanup hooks, reset message queue, clear focus/track/capture, stop running) and call it from test init/shutdown to isolate tests. Introduce reset_message_queue() to zero the global message queue and expose it in user.h. Change post_message() to avoid enqueueing duplicate (target,msg) pairs by returning early instead of nullifying existing entries. Also include <stdarg.h> in user/window.c for required vararg support.

* Fix event_loop_test failures and Windows DLL loading for tests
